sourceMovie.title: Cobra
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Cobra (1986) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lethal Weapon 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Cobra clicked for you, Lethal Weapon 2 (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Cobra?
faq.answer: Try Lethal Weapon 2, Lethal Weapon 3, Lethal Weapon 4—each shares DNA with Cobra while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Cobra?
faq.answer: Cobra (1986) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Cobra worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
